member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.BitvavoAuthenticationProvider
member.summary:
HMAC-SHA256 authentication for the Bitvavo REST API.
The signature input concatenates the millisecond-timestamp, HTTP method (uppercase), the path (including /v2 prefix and any query string) and the JSON-encoded body (empty string for GET requests). The result is the lowercase hex HMAC-SHA256 of that string with the API secret as key. See .

member.name: M:Bitvavo.Net.BitvavoAuthenticationProvider.Sign(System.String)
member.summary: Compute the HMAC-SHA256 signature for the given input using the configured API secret. Used by the websocket authenticate action.

member.name: M:Bitvavo.Net.BitvavoAuthenticationProvider.GetMillisecondsTimestamp(CryptoExchange.Net.Clients.SocketApiClient)
member.summary: Get the current millisecond timestamp string used in signatures, accounting for the configured time offset of the supplied socket client.

member.name: M:Bitvavo.Net.Clients.MessageHandlers.BitvavoRestMessageHandler.ParseErrorRateLimitResponse(System.Int32,System.Net.Http.Headers.HttpResponseHeaders,System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Bitvavo's rate-limit response carries the docs-published bitvavo-ratelimit-resetat header (epoch ms). Surface it as the rate-limit error's retry-after so the framework's RateLimitGate blocks subsequent calls until the server's reset moment instead of bursting into more 429s. Source: .

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Clients.MessageHandlers.BitvavoSocketMessageHandler
member.summary: Routes incoming websocket messages to the right query/subscription. Subscribe/unsubscribe responses are correlated by the echoed requestId; live data events are routed by event + per-message topic (e.g. market for trade updates).

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Converters.BitvavoMarketsConverter
member.summary:
Custom converter for the markets property of a Bitvavo subscribe/unsubscribe channel. Bitvavo accepts two distinct wire shapes for this field on the same channel:
An array of market names: "markets": ["BTC-EUR", "ETH-EUR"].
The bare wildcard string: "markets": "*", which subscribes to every currently listed market.
Docs:
On write, a sentinel value of a single-element string[] { "*" } is serialized as the bare string "*"; any other content (including an empty array) is serialized as a normal JSON array. On read, both shapes are accepted - a bare string is materialized as a single-element array - so round-tripping is symmetric.

member.name: M:Bitvavo.Net.Interfaces.Clients.ExchangeSocketApi.IBitvavoSocketClientExchangeApi.SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync(System.String,System.Action{CryptoExchange.Net.Objects.Sockets.DataEvent{Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoTradeUpdate}},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Subscribe to the public trade stream for a market.

Channel: trades
member.param: Cancellation token for closing this subscription

member.name: M:Bitvavo.Net.Interfaces.Clients.ExchangeSocketApi.IBitvavoSocketClientExchangeApi.SubscribeToTicker24hUpdatesAsync(System.String[],System.Action{CryptoExchange.Net.Objects.Sockets.DataEvent{Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoTicker24hUpdate}},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Subscribe to the rolling 24h ticker stream for one or more markets.
Docs:
Channel: ticker24h
member.param: Markets to subscribe to (for example BTC-EUR)
Ticker event handler. The exchange may batch multiple markets into a single update.
Cancellation token for closing this subscription

member.name: M:Bitvavo.Net.Interfaces.Clients.ExchangeSocketApi.IBitvavoSocketClientExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(System.String,System.Action{CryptoExchange.Net.Objects.Sockets.DataEvent{Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoOrderBookUpdate}},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Subscribe to incremental order book updates for a market. Emits deltas; size 0 indicates the level is removed. Combine with GET /v2/{market}/book to maintain a local book.
Docs:
Channel: book
member.param: Cancellation token for closing this subscription

member.summary:
Subscribe to the authenticated account channel for one or more markets. Emits both events (status changes) and events (executions). Authentication is established at connection level via the authenticate action; must have been called before subscribing.
Docs:
Channel: account
member.param: Markets to subscribe to (for example BTC-EUR)
Cancellation token for closing this subscription

member.summary:
Subscribe to the authenticated account channel for all currently listed markets via the documented "markets": "*" wildcard - a single subscription covers every market. Emits both events (status changes) and events (executions); the market field on each event identifies which market it belongs to. Authentication is established at connection level via the authenticate action; must have been called before subscribing.
Docs:
Channel: account
member.param: Cancellation token for closing this subscription

Client for accessing the Bitvavo websocket APIs.
Bitvavo offers two distinct websocket APIs - the regular Exchange Socket API and the low-latency Market Data Pro Socket API - exposed via and .

member.summary: Reset the cached clients for a user. This can be useful when a user changes API credentials.

member.summary: Get the Rest client for a specific user. In case the client does not exist yet it will be created and the should be provided, unless has been called prior for this user.
member.param: The credentials for the user. Required the first time a client is requested for this user unless has been called prior for this user.

member.param: The credentials for the user. Required the first time a client is requested for this user unless has been called prior for this user.

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.BitvavoTransaction
member.summary: Single entry from Bitvavo GET /v2/account/history. The set of populated currency / amount pairs varies by : trade entries (buy, sell) populate price/sent/received/fee; deposit / withdrawal entries omit the price + sent fields. Address is masked for fiat (e.g. DE32***31) and may be null for trades. .

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthRequest
member.summary:
Authentication request payload for the Bitvavo websocket Exchange API. The signature is the HMAC-SHA256 of {timestamp}GET/v2/websocket using the API secret as key, encoded as lowercase hex.
See .

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthRequest.Action
member.summary: ["action"] Always authenticate.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthRequest.Key
member.summary: ["key"] API key.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthRequest.Signature
member.summary: ["signature"] HMAC-SHA256 signature.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthRequest.Timestamp
member.summary: ["timestamp"] Unix milliseconds timestamp used in the signature.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthRequest.Window
member.summary: ["window"] Validity window in milliseconds (default 10000).

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthResponse
member.summary: Response from the Bitvavo websocket authenticate action. On success the server replies with {"event":"authenticate","authenticated":true}; on failure {"action":"authenticate","errorCode":...,"error":"..."}.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthResponse.Event
member.summary: ["event"] Set to authenticate on success

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthResponse.Action
member.summary: ["action"] Set to authenticate on action-scoped errors

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthResponse.Authenticated
member.summary: ["authenticated"] True on successful auth

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthResponse.ErrorCode
member.summary: ["errorCode"] Numeric error code on failure

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoAuthResponse.Error
member.summary: ["error"] Human-readable error message on failure

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoOrderBookUpdate
member.summary: Incremental order book update received from the Bitvavo book websocket subscription. Each with quantity 0 represents the removal of that price level. The first event after a subscribe is also a delta - to obtain a full book, fetch a snapshot via GET /v2/{market}/book and then apply incremental updates whose is greater than the snapshot nonce.

member.summary: ["market"] Market name (for example BTC-EUR)

member.summary: ["nonce"] Sequence number; increments with every order book change

member.summary: ["bids"] Bid changes; size 0 means remove level

member.summary: ["asks"] Ask changes; size 0 means remove level

member.summary: ["timestamp"] Update timestamp in nanoseconds

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSocketRequest
member.summary: Subscribe/unsubscribe request payload for the Bitvavo websocket APIs.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSocketRequest.Action
member.summary: ["action"] Action name, either subscribe or unsubscribe.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSocketRequest.RequestId
member.summary: ["requestId"] Client-supplied id; Bitvavo echoes it back in both the success ({"event":"subscribed","requestId":...}) and error ({"action":"subscribe","requestId":...,"errorCode":...}) response. Used by the framework's routing layer to match a response back to its originating query when several requests share a single websocket connection.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSocketRequest.Channels
member.summary: ["channels"] Channels to subscribe or unsubscribe from.

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionChannel
member.summary: Single channel inside a Bitvavo subscribe/unsubscribe request.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionChannel.Name
member.summary: ["name"] Channel name (for example trades, ticker24h, book).

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionChannel.Markets
member.summary: ["markets"] Markets to subscribe to (for example BTC-EUR). A single-element array containing "*" serializes as the bare string "*", which Bitvavo interprets as "every currently listed market" - see .

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionResponse
member.summary:
Response payload returned by the Bitvavo websocket server to a subscribe/unsubscribe request.
Bitvavo replies with one of two shapes:
1) Success: {"event":"subscribed","requestId":42,"subscriptions":{"trades":["BTC-EUR"]}} (or "unsubscribed" for an unsubscribe).
2) Failure: {"action":"subscribe","requestId":42,"errorCode":205,"error":"market parameter UNK-NOWN is invalid."}.
Both shapes carry the client-supplied requestId, which is what the framework uses to match a response to its originating .

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionResponse.Event
member.summary: ["event"] Set to subscribed or unsubscribed on success, absent on errors.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionResponse.Action
member.summary: ["action"] Set to subscribe or unsubscribe on action-scoped errors, absent on success.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionResponse.RequestId
member.summary: ["requestId"] Echo of the client-supplied request id.

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionResponse.ErrorCode
member.summary: ["errorCode"] Numeric error code (only present on failure).

member.name: P:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Models.Socket.BitvavoSubscriptionResponse.Error
member.summary: ["error"] Human readable error message (only present on failure).

Bitvavo exposes two distinct websocket APIs - the regular Exchange Socket API and the low-latency Market Data Pro Socket API. The same socket client connects to both via independent api-clients, configured through and .

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Sockets.BitvavoAccountSubscription
member.summary: Subscription for the Bitvavo authenticated account websocket channel. The channel emits two distinct event types - order and fill - each carrying a market field. In per-market mode the market field is used as the topic filter so a single subscription can serve multiple markets routed to the right per-market handler. In wildcard mode (markets = "*") routing happens purely by event type because events arrive for every currently listed market and a per-market filter would not match anything.

member.name: F:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Sockets.BitvavoAccountSubscription.WildcardMarket
member.summary: Sentinel used inside to indicate that the subscription should be sent to Bitvavo as the wildcard "markets": "*".
Build a subscription that listens to order and fill events for every currently listed Bitvavo market via the documented "markets": "*" wildcard. Docs:

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Sockets.BitvavoAuthQuery
member.summary:
Connection-level authenticate query for the Bitvavo Exchange Socket API.
Bitvavo's authentication response does NOT include a requestId. Routing is therefore performed by the event/action field. See .

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Sockets.BitvavoBookSubscription
member.summary: Subscription for the Bitvavo public book websocket channel. Emits incremental order book deltas; size 0 on a level indicates removal.

member.name: T:Bitvavo.Net.Objects.Sockets.BitvavoQuery
member.summary:
Subscribe/unsubscribe query for the Bitvavo websocket API.
Bitvavo replies to a subscribe/unsubscribe with either:
a success event - {"event":"subscribed","requestId":N,...} or {"event":"unsubscribed","requestId":N,...}; or
an action-scoped error - {"action":"subscribe","requestId":N,"errorCode":...,"error":"..."}.
Both shapes echo the client-supplied requestId; this query assigns a fresh id on construction and routes by it, so concurrent subscribes on a single connection are disambiguated. The ack is consumed silently on success and surfaced as a on failure.

member.summary: Subscription for the Bitvavo public trades websocket channel.
